title: epithecia trulla
side_effects: Epithecia trulla can have side effects if used improperly, such as allergic reactions, skin irritation, and respiratory problems. Ingesting the plant can cause nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, as well as interact with certain medications and exacerbate underlying health conditions. Improper use of Epithecia trulla can also lead to headaches, dizziness, and fatigue, particularly if the plant is not properly dried or processed. In severe cases, prolonged exposure to the plant's toxins can result in kidney or liver damage.
